Site.tagline: Building fast products with React, Next.js and TypeScript
Site.shortBio: Frontend developer with 3+ years of experience building products — from design systems to complex SPAs.
bio: For 3+ years I've been designing and building web products. My core expertise is React and Next.js, plus a healthy obsession with clean architecture, strict typing, and pixel-perfect UI.

I've spent an embarrassing amount of time nudging a box-shadow half a pixel to the left, and I regret nothing. If a button doesn't feel right on click, I will find out why — usually at 1am, alone, with devtools open.

Accessibility and performance matter to me as much as looks: a beautiful interface that breaks for a screen reader or chokes on a slow connection isn't finished, it's just a pretty screenshot. Good UI is when something complicated feels effortless.

I've shipped a realtime pizza-delivery tracker, argued with WebSockets that dropped mid-delivery, and built a video platform that had to survive real traffic — not the 'works on my localhost' kind. TypeScript has saved me from myself more times than I'd like to admit.

Off the clock I read changelogs the way other people read novels, hoard animation libraries I'll 'definitely use someday', and occasionally explain to non-developers why a website is not, in fact, 'just HTML'.

Footer.builtWith: Built with Next.js, Tailwind CSS and Motion.

Home.projectsDescription: A few case studies I'm proud of. The full list is on the projects page.

TechStack.title: Technology stack
TechStack.description: The frontend tools I use in my work — from frameworks to dev tooling.

description.typescript: A typed layer over JavaScript: catches errors before runtime, powers autocomplete and makes refactoring safe.
description.html5: The standard markup language for the web — semantic structure, accessibility, and native browser APIs.
description.css3: Stylesheets for the web: from layout (flexbox, grid) to animations and responsive design.
description.react: A library for declarative component-based UI — the foundation of most modern SPAs and the base of the frontend stack.
description.nextjs: A React framework with SSR/SSG, file-based routing and server components — for fast, SEO-friendly production apps.
description.javascript: The language of the web — runs in the browser and on the server. The base the entire frontend toolchain stands on.
description.scss: A CSS preprocessor with variables, nesting, mixins and functions — keeps stylesheets maintainable at scale.
description.tailwind: A utility-first CSS framework — build UI right in the markup, no file-switching and no dead CSS.
description.redux: Predictable state management for complex client state with minimal boilerplate and great dev tooling.
description.redux-toolkit: The official, opinionated Redux toolset: simplified store setup, RTK Query, and reduced boilerplate.
description.mobx: Reactive state management with observable values and automatic derivation tracking — minimal boilerplate.
description.react-hook-form: Performant form library for React with uncontrolled inputs and schema-based validation.
description.zod: A TypeScript-first schema validation library with precise type inference.
description.tanstack-query: Async state management for server data: caching, deduplication, background refetching and pagination.
description.apollo: A comprehensive state management library for GraphQL: queries, mutations, and a normalized cache.
description.graphql: A query language for APIs — fetch exactly the data you need in a single request.
description.material-ui: A comprehensive React component library implementing Material Design — pre-built UI blocks and theming.
description.shadcn: A collection of beautifully designed, accessible React components built on Radix UI and Tailwind.
description.radix: A headless UI component library — unstyled, accessible primitives for building custom design systems.
description.motion: An animation library for React: declarative transitions, gestures and layout animations that respect prefers-reduced-motion.
description.tanstack-virtual: A headless virtualizer for rendering large lists efficiently — only the visible rows are in the DOM.
description.react-router: The standard routing library for React: nested routes, loaders, actions and client-side navigation.
description.git: Version control — change history, branches and collaboration on code without the chaos.
description.prettier: An opinionated code formatter — consistent style across the codebase, no more style discussions.
description.eslint: A static analysis tool for finding and fixing problems in JavaScript and TypeScript code.
description.figma: A collaborative design tool for UI/UX — vector editing, prototyping, and design systems in the browser.
description.capacitor: A cross-platform native runtime for building mobile apps from web code — iOS, Android, and the web from one codebase.
description.react-native: A framework for building native mobile apps using React — share business logic across platforms with native UI.
description.claude: An AI assistant by Anthropic — helpful, honest, and harmless. Great for code review, reasoning, and technical writing.
description.chatgpt: A conversational AI by OpenAI — assists with coding, content generation, brainstorming, and problem-solving.
description.grok: An AI assistant by xAI — real-time knowledge, code generation, and technical problem-solving with a direct style.

NotFound.title: Page not found

where-is-pizza.summary: A full-stack pizza-shop storefront: catalog, cart, checkout, JWT authentication.
description: A pizza shop monorepo: a Next.js client (catalog with filters, cart, checkout, an order-confirmation page, an account area) and a separate Express server.
The backend runs on Express + Prisma over SQLite: routes and controllers for authentication (JWT, bcrypt), cart, orders, and product filtering.
On the client — Redux Toolkit with redux-persist for the cart, TanStack Query for server data, react-hook-form with Zod for forms, and swiper/keen-slider for catalog carousels.

kicks-sneakers.summary: A sneaker e-commerce store built on Feature-Sliced Design: filterable catalog, auth, user roles.
description: A sneaker store: a catalog with price and category filters, product cards, and sign-up/sign-in with separate admin and regular user roles.

Frontend on React + TypeScript, structured with Feature-Sliced Design (app / pages / widgets / features / entities / shared), state via Redux Toolkit with redux-persist, server data via TanStack Query.
The backend is mocked with json-server, forms use react-hook-form with Zod, notifications via react-hot-toast, plus loading skeletons and an error boundary for UI resilience.

share-your-tales.summary: A mobile social network for stories: feed, likes, sharing, dark mode. Apollo GraphQL + Expo.
description: A mobile social app for posting short stories: a feed, profile, likes and bookmarks, sharing to external platforms, and photo uploads from the gallery or camera.
React Native on Expo, a GraphQL layer via Apollo Client, navigation combining a stack, a drawer, and tabs from React Navigation, forms via react-hook-form with Zod validation.
Both dark and light themes are supported, along with a custom splash screen and adaptive icons; gestures run on react-native-gesture-handler and reanimated.

bar-menu-animations.summary: An animated bar landing page: parallax, a drinks slider, and frame-by-frame GSAP animations.
description: A landing page for a bar's cocktail menu: a hero with parallax decorative elements, a drinks slider, a testimonials section — a single-pager dense with animation.
React 19 with the React Compiler enabled, all animation done with GSAP and @gsap/react, responsiveness via react-responsive, styling with Tailwind CSS v4 through its Vite plugin.
Built on Vite with Rolldown and a Babel plugin for the React Compiler — an experimental setup aimed at maximum build performance.

eatly.summary: A restaurant brand landing page: menu, testimonials, pricing, and animated modals.
description: A restaurant marketing site: a hero, a menu section with dish cards, testimonials, delivery subscription pricing, and modals with dish details.
Next.js App Router, UI built on a combination of MUI and Radix UI primitives (dialog, slider, labels), transition animations via Framer Motion.
State (cart, menu filters) is handled with Zustand, forms (booking, ordering) with react-hook-form, styling with Tailwind CSS on top of the MUI theme.

evogym.summary: A gym landing page with Framer Motion animations, a contact form, and a partners section.
description: A landing page for a fitness brand: a hero with animated graphics, a benefits section, a partners block, and a contact form.
React and TypeScript on Vite, animations via Framer Motion, the contact form via react-hook-form, styling with Tailwind CSS and a custom scrollbar.
The project is fully typed and deployed to GitHub Pages via gh-pages.

nexcent-tailwind.summary: A marketing landing page on React and Tailwind: partners section, blog, service cards, CTA blocks.
description: A single-page landing with hero, benefits, blog, and partners/clients sections — a typical marketing site structure for a club or subscription service.
Built with React and Vite, styled with Tailwind CSS and Flowbite React components, icons via react-icons, smooth section scrolling via react-scroll.
Deployment is automated with gh-pages, serving the site as static content on GitHub Pages.

hangman.summary: A Hangman game with keyboard input and SVG rendering of the figure based on the mistake count.
description: A classic Hangman game: a random word from a word list, letter input from the keyboard or an on-screen keyboard, the figure drawing gets more complete with each mistake.
React and TypeScript on Vite; game state — guessed and incorrect letters, win/loss — is derived through hooks without external stores.
A standalone project for practicing keyboard event handling and conditional SVG rendering.
